title: physostigma coriaceum
intro: Physostigma coriaceum, also known as the calabar bean, is a plant species native to West Africa that contains the toxic alkaloid physostigmine. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and snake bites, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Physostigma coriaceum include its ability to improve cognitive function, alleviate symptoms of Parkinson's disease, and treat conditions such as glaucoma and myasthenia gravis. Herbal preparations of Physostigma coriaceum, such as infusions and decoctions, have been used in traditional medicine, but caution is advised due to the plant's toxic properties, and it should only be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
